evals.prompt: I have two folders of PDFs, one for federated learning and one for skill discovery. Build a reusable literature review packet with per-paper notes, separate LLM reviewer checks, cross-direction comparison, research gaps, and ranked improvement ideas. Use an OpenAI-compatible API from environment variables.
evals.expected_output: A result directory containing extracted texts, notes for both directions, review JSONL files, five synthesis Markdown files, a manifest, and an audit report.
expectations: The workflow uses one LLM call for note generation and a separate LLM call for quality review.
The workflow uses a separate LLM call to review synthesis, cross-analysis, research-gap, and improvement-idea documents.
The output includes per-paper notes for both directions.
The output includes cross_direction_analysis.md, research_gaps.md, and improvement_ideas_ranked.md.
The output can be validated by scripts/validate_outputs.py.

evals.prompt: Turn my two research directions into a research-gap study, but make sure the notes are not generic summaries. I need assumptions, failure modes, concrete methods, and non-template implications for each paper.
evals.expected_output: The skill instructs the model to use the structured note schema and reviewer gate that rejects generic importance, vague methods, and repeated implications.
expectations: The note schema requires background, core challenge, step-by-step method, evidence, assumptions, failure modes, and agenda-specific implications.
The reviewer checks whether importance and implications are generic.
The workflow accepts a user agenda or agenda file so final gaps and ideas can obey explicit priorities and exclusions.
The batch quality gate retries failed notes using reviewer feedback.

evals.prompt: I want to package this workflow as a skill and verify it. It should include scripts, not just a Markdown instruction file.
evals.expected_output: The skill includes executable scripts, an output validator, eval definitions, and can pass skill-creator quick validation.
